Attrs.prompt:
Implement Stripe billing to make all Phase 6 tests pass. Work in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create these files under firebase/functions/src/billing/:

1. stripe.ts — Stripe webhook handlers:
   - handleStripeWebhook(req) -> { received: boolean }
   - Verify signature: stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(body, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET)
   - invoice.paid -> update clients/{clientId} billing_status = 'active' (lookup by stripe_customer_id)
   - invoice.payment_failed -> billing_status = 'suspended' immediately, no grace period
   - customer.subscription.deleted -> billing_status = 'cancelled'
   - Unknown events -> return 200, ignore
   - Use stripe npm package

2. gate.ts — Billing gate module:
   - checkBilling(clientId) -> { allowed: boolean, reason?: string }
   - Fetch client from Firestore
   - Check billing_status === 'active'
   - Check monthly usage < monthly_query_limit
   - Refactor auth middleware to use this module

3. usage.ts — Usage tracking:
   - recordUsage(clientId) -> increments usage/{clientId}.months.{YYYY-MM}.count using FieldValue.increment(1)
   - getUsage(clientId) -> returns current month count
   - resetMonthlyQuotas() — scheduled function (0 0 1 * *) for monthly reset (or let month key roll over)

Modify firebase/functions/src/index.ts:
- Export Stripe webhook endpoint
- Export scheduled quota reset function (onSchedule from firebase-functions/v2/scheduler)

Commit after each component with conventional commits.

Quality checks after each commit:
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx jest --verbose

Implement the Go CLI to make all Phase 2 tests pass. Work in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create these files under cli/:

1. cmd/root.go — Cobra root command with version flag, config path ~/.config/askkaya/
2. main.go — calls cmd.Execute()

3. internal/auth/client.go — Firebase Auth REST client:
   - AuthClient struct with APIKey, HTTPClient
   - SignIn(email, password) -> (*AuthTokens, error)
   - RefreshToken(refreshToken) -> (*AuthTokens, error)
   - AuthTokens struct: IDToken, RefreshToken, ExpiresIn, ExpiresAt

4. internal/auth/keychain.go — OS keychain token storage:
   - StoreTokens, LoadTokens, ClearTokens using github.com/zalando/go-keyring
   - Service name: 'askkaya'

5. internal/api/client.go — Firebase Functions HTTP client:
   - APIClient struct with BaseURL, ClientID, Auth, HTTPClient
   - Query(question) -> (*QueryResponse, error) — POST /api/query with Bearer token + X-Client-ID
   - HealthCheck() -> error — GET /api/health
   - Auto-refresh on 401, structured error on 403

6. cmd/auth.go — auth login (prompts email/password or takes flags), auth logout (clears keychain)
7. cmd/query.go — query 'question' sends query, prints formatted response
8. cmd/status.go — shows auth status, billing, connectivity

9. internal/tui/app.go — Root Bubble Tea model with state machine: LoginScreen -> QueryScreen -> LoadingScreen -> ResponseScreen
10. internal/tui/login.go — Auth TUI
11. internal/tui/query.go — Query input + response display
12. internal/tui/status.go — Status display
13. internal/tui/styles.go — Lipgloss styles: response box (bordered), confidence indicator (green>0.7, yellow 0.4-0.7, red<0.4), sources (dimmed), error (red bordered)

14. internal/heartbeat/daemon.go — Background health check (configurable interval, default 5 min)
15. internal/heartbeat/launchd.go — macOS launchd plist management for ~/Library/LaunchAgents/
16. cmd/heartbeat.go — heartbeat start/stop/install/uninstall commands

17. cmd/skill.go — OpenClaw skill registration:
    - 'askkaya skill register' outputs skill definition JSON
    - 'askkaya skill query question' accepts piped input, returns JSON response

Commit after each major component with conventional commits.

Quality checks after each commit:
- Run: cd cli && go vet ./...
- Run: cd cli && go test ./... -v

Implement the Firebase backend to make all Phase 1 tests pass. Work in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create these files under firebase/functions/src/:

1. middleware/auth.ts — Auth middleware:
   - Extract Bearer token from Authorization header
   - Verify token with admin.auth().verifyIdToken(token)
   - Extract client ID from X-Client-ID header
   - Fetch client document from Firestore clients/{clientId}
   - Check billing_status === 'active' — if not, return 403 'Subscription inactive' (hard cutoff, no grace)
   - Attach client data to req.client
   - Increment usage counter for current month (fire-and-forget)
   - Call next()

2. services/embeddings.ts — OpenAI embedding service:
   - generateEmbedding(text: string): Promise<number[]> — uses text-embedding-3-small
   - generateEmbeddings(texts: string[]): Promise<number[][]> — batch
   - cosineSimilarity(a: number[], b: number[]): number
   - Uses openai npm package, API key from process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY

3. services/rag.ts — RAG retrieval:
   - retrieveContext(clientId, queryEmbedding, setupContext): Promise<RetrievalResult[]>
   - Search global KB: knowledge_base/global/articles
   - Search client KB: knowledge_base/clients/{clientId}/articles
   - Per-client articles get 30% score boost
   - setup_context tag matches get additional weight
   - Return top-5 results above 0.3 threshold

4. services/generation.ts — Claude API generation:
   - generateResponse(query, context[], clientName): Promise<{ text, confidence, sources, shouldEscalate }>
   - Uses claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929 via @anthropic-ai/sdk
   - System prompt: 'You are AskKaya, a support assistant for {clientName}. Answer using ONLY the provided context.'
   - Confidence < 0.4 triggers escalation

5. services/tickets.ts — Escalation CRUD:
   - createTicket, getTicket, answerTicket, listPendingTickets, closeTicket
   - Firestore collection: escalations/{ticketId}

6. api/query.ts — Query endpoint (Cloud Function v2 onRequest):
   - Wire: auth middleware -> embed query -> RAG retrieve -> generate -> optional escalation -> return response
   - POST only, 400 if no query

7. api/health.ts — Health endpoint:
   - Returns { status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO string }

8. index.ts — Export query and health functions

Commit after each major service. Use conventional commits:
- 'feat: add auth middleware with billing gate hard cutoff'
- 'feat: add OpenAI embedding service with batch support'
- 'feat: add RAG retrieval with client context boosting'
- 'feat: add Claude-powered response generation'
- 'feat: add escalation ticket service'
- 'feat: add query endpoint wiring full pipeline'

Quality checks after each commit:
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx jest --verbose

Implement the KB ingestion pipeline to make all Phase 3 tests pass. Work in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create these files under firebase/functions/src/processing/:

1. webhook.ts — GitHub webhook receiver (Cloud Function v2 onRequest):
   - Verify signature using HMAC-SHA256 with process.env.GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET
   - Parse push event payload, filter for added/modified .md files
   - Fetch file content from GitHub API
   - Map directory structure to target: global/ -> 'global', clients/{name}/ -> { clientId }
   - Export: handleGitHubWebhook(req) -> { files: FileToProcess[], skipped: number }

2. parser.ts — Markdown structure extraction:
   - Extract YAML frontmatter (between --- markers)
   - Parse heading hierarchy (# through ####)
   - Detect document type by content patterns
   - Export: parseMarkdown(content, filename) -> ParsedDocument

3. chunker.ts — Semantic text chunking:
   - Target ~500 tokens per chunk (rough: word count / 0.75)
   - Respect section boundaries, split long sections at paragraph breaks
   - Include heading breadcrumb context in each chunk
   - 50 token overlap for continuity
   - Export: chunkDocument(doc: ParsedDocument, sourceRef: string) -> Chunk[]

4. embedder.ts — Batch embedding generation:
   - Batch chunks into groups of 20 max
   - Use embeddings service (generateEmbeddings) from services/embeddings.ts
   - Retry failed batches once
   - Export: embedChunks(chunks: Chunk[]) -> EmbeddedChunk[]

5. articulator.ts — LLM article generation:
   - Use Claude Haiku (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001) for speed/cost
   - System prompt: 'You are a technical writer creating a KB article from raw notes. Create: title, 1-2 sentence summary, well-organized content with headers, client_context tags.'
   - Export: articulateArticle(chunks: EmbeddedChunk[], sourceRef: string) -> KnowledgeArticle (without id/timestamps)

6. indexer.ts — Firestore storage:
   - Store in correct collection based on target
   - Generate ID from title slug + timestamp
   - Upsert: if existing article has same source_ref, update it
   - Store raw dump in raw_dumps/ subcollection
   - Export: indexArticle(article, target) -> string (article ID)

Modify firebase/functions/src/index.ts to export the GitHub webhook function.

Commit after each component with conventional commits.

Quality checks after each commit:
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx jest --verbose

Implement the notification system to make all Phase 4 tests pass. Work in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create these files under firebase/functions/src/notify/:

1. telegram.ts — Telegram Bot API integration:
   - formatEscalationAlert(escalation: Escalation) -> string (formatted message with client name, query, context)
   - sendMessage(chatId: string, text: string) -> Promise<void> (POST to https://api.telegram.org/bot{TOKEN}/sendMessage)
   - handleTelegramUpdate(update) — process reply: extract answer, call answerTicket(), trigger auto-learn (create KB article from answer, generate embedding, index in client KB), send confirmation
   - Env vars: process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, process.env.TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID

2. imessage.ts — iMessage bridge (fallback):
   - sendIMMessage(phoneNumber, message) -> Promise<boolean>
   - HTTP call to configurable webhook URL for local bridge

3. router.ts — Channel priority routing:
   - sendNotification(escalation: Escalation) -> Promise<{ sent: boolean, channel: string }>
   - Try telegram first, then imessage, then email
   - Record channel on escalation Firestore document

Modify firebase/functions/src/index.ts:
- Add Firestore onCreate trigger for escalations/{ticketId}:
  import { onDocumentCreated } from 'firebase-functions/v2/firestore'
  On new escalation, call sendNotification(escalation)
- Add Telegram webhook endpoint (onRequest)

Auto-learn pipeline (triggered when answerTicket is called):
1. Take answer text + original query
2. Create KB article: title from query, content from answer
3. Generate embedding via embeddings service
4. Index in client KB collection via indexer

Commit after each component with conventional commits.

Quality checks after each commit:
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx jest --verbose

15. app/analytics/page.tsx — Analytics with recharts

Run full E2E integration test across all components in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create tests/e2e/full-pipeline.test.ts (or shell script) that tests against Firebase emulator:

1. Setup: Seed Firestore with client (active billing, setup_context: ['vapi']) and global KB articles
2. CLI Auth: Verify askkaya auth login works with test credentials against emulator
3. Query (high confidence): POST /api/query with VAPI question -> response with sources
4. Query (low confidence): POST /api/query with off-topic question -> 'Let me check with Kaya' + escalation
5. Escalation: Verify ticket exists in Firestore with status 'pending'
6. Notification: Verify Telegram notification triggered (check logs)
7. Auto-learn: Simulate Telegram reply -> ticket updated, new KB article created
8. KB ingestion: POST GitHub webhook with .md file -> article appears in Firestore
9. Web dashboard: cd web && npx next build (verify build succeeds)
10. Billing cutoff: Suspend client in Firestore -> query returns 403

Run with: firebase emulators:exec 'npx jest tests/e2e/ --verbose' --only firestore,auth

Report outcome=SUCCESS if all pass, outcome=FAIL if any fail.

Polish and harden the AskKaya codebase in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

1. Tighten Firestore rules to least-privilege (review firebase/firestore/firestore.rules)
2. Tune RAG parameters in firebase/functions/src/services/rag.ts: similarity threshold (0.3), top-K (5), client boost (30%)
3. Tune confidence thresholds in firebase/functions/src/services/generation.ts: escalation (0.65), critical (0.4)
4. Add rate limiting to query endpoint (100 req/min per client)
5. Verify PII redaction in responses (no email/phone in generated text)
6. Add structured logging (JSON format for Cloud Logging) across all Cloud Functions
7. Clean up TODO comments

Quality checks:
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx jest --verbose
- Run: cd cli && go vet ./... && go test ./... -v
- Run: cd web && npx tsc --noEmit && npx next build

Commit: git commit -m 'chore: polish and harden for production'

6. Create operator runbook covering: client onboarding, billing lifecycle, escalation workflow, KB management

- All tests still pass

Commit: git commit -m 'chore: prepare release artifacts and documentation'

Set up the AskKaya monorepo in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create this directory structure:
- cli/ — Go module (Cobra + Bubble Tea + Lipgloss)
- firebase/functions/ — TypeScript Cloud Functions v2
- firebase/firestore/ — Firestore rules + indexes
- web/ — Next.js 14 app
- config/.env.example — env var template
- docs/ — documentation
- package.json — monorepo root
- pnpm-workspace.yaml — pnpm workspaces config
- tsconfig.base.json — shared TS config

Steps:
1. Create root package.json with pnpm workspaces pointing to firebase/functions and web.
2. Create pnpm-workspace.yaml listing firebase/functions and web.
3. Create tsconfig.base.json with strict TS settings.
4. Initialize Go module: cd cli && go mod init github.com/askkaya/cli
5. Install Go deps: go get github.com/spf13/cobra@latest github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea@latest github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss@latest github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles@latest github.com/zalando/go-keyring@latest
6. Initialize firebase/functions: cd firebase/functions && pnpm init, then pnpm add firebase-admin firebase-functions openai @anthropic-ai/sdk stripe. Dev deps: pnpm add -D typescript jest ts-jest @types/jest @types/node.
7. Create firebase/functions/tsconfig.json extending base config, target ES2021, module NodeNext.
8. Create firebase/functions/src/index.ts as empty Cloud Functions entry point.
9. Create config/.env.example with: OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID, STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, FIREBASE_API_KEY, GITHUB_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
10. Verify: cd cli && go build ./... (should succeed). cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit (should succeed).

Quality checks:
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit
- Run: cd cli && go vet ./...

Commit: git add -A && git commit -m 'feat: initialize AskKaya monorepo with Go CLI, Firebase functions, and Next.js'

Write ALL failing tests for Stripe billing integration in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create these test files under firebase/functions/__tests__/:

1. unit/stripe.test.ts — Stripe webhook handlers:
   - Verifies Stripe signature using stripe.webhooks.constructEvent()
   - invoice.paid -> sets client billing_status = 'active'
   - invoice.payment_failed -> sets billing_status = 'suspended' immediately (NO grace period)
   - customer.subscription.deleted -> sets billing_status = 'cancelled'
   - Maps Stripe customer ID to client ID via stripe_customer_id field
   - Ignores unknown event types (returns 200)
   - Rejects invalid signatures (400)
   Import from: '../../src/billing/stripe'

2. unit/gate.test.ts — Billing gate:
   - checkBilling(clientId) returns { allowed: boolean, reason?: string }
   - active -> { allowed: true }
   - suspended -> { allowed: false, reason: 'Subscription inactive' }
   - Quota exceeded -> { allowed: false, reason: 'Monthly query limit reached' }
   Import from: '../../src/billing/gate'

3. unit/usage.test.ts — Usage tracking:
   - recordUsage(clientId) increments query count for current month
   - getUsage(clientId) returns current month usage
   - Usage key format: months.{YYYY-MM}.count
   Import from: '../../src/billing/usage'

4. integration/billing.test.ts — Full billing integration:
   - Create client with billing_status 'active', monthly_query_limit 10
   - Send query -> succeeds
   - Simulate invoice.payment_failed webhook -> billing_status = 'suspended'
   - Send query -> 403 'Subscription inactive'
   - Simulate invoice.paid webhook -> billing_status = 'active'
   - Send query -> succeeds
   - Send 10 queries to exhaust quota
   - Send 11th -> rejected 'Monthly query limit reached'

All tests MUST fail initially.

   - Query(question) sends POST to /api/query with Authorization and X-Client-ID headers
   - Returns QueryResponse{Text, Confidence, Sources, Escalated}
   - HealthCheck() sends GET to /api/health
   - Auto-refreshes token on 401 (one retry)
   - Returns structured error on 403 (billing suspended)

4. internal/tui/app_test.go — Bubble Tea TUI tests using teatest:

   - Query screen accepts text input, sends query, displays response

   Use github.com/charmbracelet/x/exp/teatest

5. internal/heartbeat/daemon_test.go — Heartbeat tests:
   - Daemon.Start() begins periodic health checks
   - Daemon.Stop() stops the loop
   - Detects expired auth token, logs warning
   - Detects suspended billing, logs warning

All tests MUST fail initially.

   - Preserves section boundaries
   - Each chunk includes heading context breadcrumb ('Setup > VAPI > Webhooks')
   - 50 token overlap between chunks
   - Handles very short docs (single chunk)
   - Handles very long sections (splits at paragraph boundaries)
   Types: Chunk { text, headingContext, index, totalChunks, sourceRef }
   Import from: '../../src/processing/chunker'

4. unit/embedder.test.ts — Batch embedder:
   - embedChunks(chunks[]) generates embeddings for all chunks
   - Batches API calls (max 20 per batch)
   - Returns EmbeddedChunk (Chunk + embedding: number[])
   - Handles partial failures with retry (2 attempts)
   Import from: '../../src/processing/embedder'

5. unit/articulator.test.ts — Article articulator:
   - articulateArticle(chunks[], sourceRef) generates structured KB article via Claude Haiku
   - Returns { title, summary, content, client_context: string[], source_refs, embedding, auto_generated: true }
   - Groups related chunks before articulation
   Import from: '../../src/processing/articulator'

6. unit/indexer.test.ts — Firestore indexer:
   - indexArticle(article, 'global') stores in knowledge_base/global/articles/{id}
   - indexArticle(article, { clientId }) stores in knowledge_base/clients/{clientId}/articles/{id}
   - Generates unique ID from title slug + timestamp
   - Upserts if source_ref matches
   Import from: '../../src/processing/indexer'

7. integration/kb-ingest.test.ts — Full pipeline integration:
   - Simulate GitHub webhook push with .md file
   - Verify article appears in Firestore with embedding, title, summary
   - Verify RAG retrieval finds the new article

All tests MUST fail initially.

Quality checks:
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx jest --verbose (expect FAIL for new tests)

Commit: git add firebase/functions/__tests__/ && git commit -m 'test: add failing tests for KB ingestion pipeline Phase 3'

Write ALL failing tests for the notification system in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create these test files under firebase/functions/__tests__/:

1. unit/telegram.test.ts — Telegram bot:
   - formatEscalationAlert(escalation) returns formatted message with client name, query, context tags
   - sendMessage(chatId, text) calls Telegram Bot API POST https://api.telegram.org/bot{TOKEN}/sendMessage
   - handleTelegramUpdate(update) processes reply messages: extracts answer, calls answerTicket, triggers auto-learn, sends confirmation
   - Handles Telegram API errors with 1 retry
   Env vars: TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN, TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID
   Import from: '../../src/notify/telegram'

2. unit/router.test.ts — Notification router:
   - sendNotification(escalation) tries channels in order: telegram -> imessage -> email
   - If primary succeeds, skips fallbacks
   - If primary fails, tries next channel
   - Records which channel was used on escalation document
   - Returns { sent: boolean, channel: string }
   Import from: '../../src/notify/router'

3. integration/escalation.test.ts — Full escalation flow:
   - Create escalation ticket in Firestore
   - Verify notification trigger fires
   - Simulate Telegram reply (call webhook handler)
   - Verify ticket status updated to 'answered'
   - Verify new KB article created from the answer (auto-learn)
   - Verify answer retrievable via RAG

All tests MUST fail initially.

Quality checks:
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit
- Run: cd firebase/functions && npx jest --verbose (expect FAIL for new tests)

Commit: git add firebase/functions/__tests__/ && git commit -m 'test: add failing tests for notification system Phase 4'

Write ALL failing tests for the Next.js admin dashboard in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Create these test files under web/__tests__/:

1. login.test.tsx — Auth + login:
   - Login page renders email/password form
   - Firebase Auth signInWithEmailAndPassword called on submit
   - Redirects to /dashboard on success
   - Shows error on invalid credentials
   - Protected routes redirect to /login if unauthenticated
   - Rejects non-admin users (missing admin:true custom claim)

2. dashboard.test.tsx — Dashboard page:
   - Renders recent query count (last 24h)
   - Renders active escalation count
   - Renders client count
   - Renders billing summary (active vs suspended)
   Uses Server Components fetching from Firestore

3. clients.test.tsx — Client management:
   - Client list shows all clients with name, status, setup_context tags
   - Client detail shows full info + per-client KB article count + recent queries
   - Create form with name, email, setup_context tags
   - Edit updates Firestore document

4. kb.test.tsx — KB browser:
   - Lists all articles (global + per-client) with title, summary, source
   - Search filters by title/content text match
   - Filter by client or global
   - Article viewer shows full content with metadata
   - Re-process button triggers re-indexing

5. escalations.test.tsx — Escalation queue:
   - Lists tickets filtered by status (pending/answered/closed)
   - Ticket detail shows client name, query, context, timestamps
   - Reply form submits answer, updates status, triggers auto-learn
   - Close button marks ticket as closed

6. analytics.test.tsx — Analytics page:
   - Renders query volume chart (per day, last 30 days)
   - Renders confidence distribution histogram
   - Renders escalation rate percentage
   - Renders top queried topics

Tech: Next.js 14 App Router, React Testing Library, jest, Firebase client + admin SDK.

All tests MUST fail initially.

Quality checks:
- Run: cd web && npx tsc --noEmit
- Run: cd web && npx jest --verbose (expect FAIL)

Commit: git add web/__tests__/ && git commit -m 'test: add failing tests for Next.js admin dashboard Phase 5'

Verify all Phase 6 billing tests pass in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Run these commands:
1. cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit (zero errors)
2. cd firebase/functions && npx jest __tests__/unit/stripe.test.ts __tests__/unit/gate.test.ts __tests__/unit/usage.test.ts --verbose
3. cd firebase/functions && firebase emulators:exec 'npx jest __tests__/integration/billing.test.ts --verbose' --only firestore,auth

All tests must pass. Report outcome=SUCCESS if every test passes, outcome=FAIL if any test fails.

Verify all Phase 2 Go CLI tests pass in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Run these commands:
1. cd cli && go vet ./... (must pass with zero warnings)
2. cd cli && go build -o askkaya . (must compile successfully)
3. cd cli && go test ./... -v (all unit tests must pass)
4. cd cli && ./askkaya --help (smoke test: must print help text)

All tests must pass. Report outcome=SUCCESS if every test passes, outcome=FAIL if any test fails.

If tests fail, report which tests failed and why.

Verify all Phase 1 Firebase backend tests pass in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Run these commands:
1. cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit (TypeScript compilation must succeed with zero errors)
2. cd firebase/functions && npx jest __tests__/unit/ --verbose (all unit tests must pass)
3. cd firebase/functions && firebase emulators:exec 'npx jest __tests__/integration/query-pipeline.test.ts --verbose' --only firestore,auth (integration tests against emulator)

All tests must pass. Report outcome=SUCCESS if every test passes, outcome=FAIL if any test fails.

If tests fail, report which tests failed and why.

Verify all Phase 3 KB pipeline tests pass in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Run these commands:
1. cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit (zero errors)
2. cd firebase/functions && npx jest __tests__/unit/webhook.test.ts __tests__/unit/parser.test.ts __tests__/unit/chunker.test.ts __tests__/unit/embedder.test.ts __tests__/unit/articulator.test.ts __tests__/unit/indexer.test.ts --verbose
3. cd firebase/functions && firebase emulators:exec 'npx jest __tests__/integration/kb-ingest.test.ts --verbose' --only firestore,auth

All tests must pass. Report outcome=SUCCESS if every test passes, outcome=FAIL if any test fails.

Verify all Phase 4 notification tests pass in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Run these commands:
1. cd firebase/functions && npx tsc --noEmit (zero errors)
2. cd firebase/functions && npx jest __tests__/unit/telegram.test.ts __tests__/unit/router.test.ts --verbose
3. cd firebase/functions && firebase emulators:exec 'npx jest __tests__/integration/escalation.test.ts --verbose' --only firestore,auth

All tests must pass. Report outcome=SUCCESS if every test passes, outcome=FAIL if any test fails.

Verify all Phase 5 web dashboard tests pass in the run working directory (context key run.working_dir).

Run these commands:
1. cd web && npx tsc --noEmit (zero errors)
2. cd web && npx jest --verbose (all component and route tests must pass)
3. cd web && npx next build (build must succeed without errors)

All tests must pass and build must succeed. Report outcome=SUCCESS if everything passes, outcome=FAIL if anything fails.
